hero.title: Choose the route that matches the job.
hero.subtitle: Use public channels for partnerships and general contact. Once signed in, the same page reveals a ticket lane with attachments, verification, and follow-up history.
hero.panelDescription: Direct contact stays public. Ticket handling appears only for signed-in users and keeps every reply in one thread.
hero.responseDescription: Tickets can move through open, in progress, waiting for user, resolved, and closed states.
hero.verificationDescription: Every user submission and reply is checked through Turnstile and attachment ownership validation.

contactMethods.title: Start with the fastest route.
contactMethods.emailDescription: Best for detailed issues, file-based reports, and async coordination.
contactMethods.phoneDescription: Best for urgent follow-up during active support hours.
contactMethods.addressDescription: CarbonTrack core team operations and collaboration base.

website_bug.title: Website bug
website_bug.description: Broken flows, upload failures, access errors, and visual regressions.

business_issue.title: Business issue
business_issue.description: Partnership coordination, rollout logistics, and operational follow-up.

feature_request.title: Feature request
feature_request.description: Ideas that improve daily tracking, review, or operational workflows.

account.title: Account issue
account.description: Sign-in, verification, profile, permission, or account state questions.

feedback.title: Open a ticket
feedback.subtitle: Create it once, then continue from the ticket page.

placeholders.subject: Short summary of the issue
placeholders.content: Describe what happened, what you expected, and what support should verify.

attachments.uploadRequired: If you selected files, upload them before submitting the ticket or reply.
attachments.uploadFailedBlocking: Some attachments failed to upload. Retry or remove them before submitting.

recent.title: Recent tickets
recent.subtitle: Resume the thread or inspect the latest reply.
recent.empty: You do not have any tickets yet. New submissions will appear here.

userList.title: Support threads tied to your account
userList.subtitle: Open a request from help, then continue the conversation here.
userList.queueSubtitle: Sorted by the latest activity time.
userList.emptyDescription: Use the help page to submit the first request.

thread.summarySubtitle: Scan status and last handling time quickly.
thread.feedbackSubtitle: Once the ticket is finished, rate each handling agent and leave a note.
thread.feedbackEmpty: No handling agents are available for feedback on this ticket.
thread.feedbackHint: You can submit now and still edit the feedback later.
thread.feedbackUpdateHint: Feedback is already saved. You can update it again.
thread.feedbackCommentPlaceholder: Share response quality, handling speed, or suggestions.
thread.replySubtitle: New replies notify the support mailbox.
thread.replyPlaceholder: Share the next detail, progress update, or requested evidence.
thread.closedHint: Closed tickets no longer accept new user replies.

portal.title: Support workbench
portal.subtitle: Work the live queue, answer conversations, and keep SLA-critical tickets in view.

nav.queueDescription: Search, filter, and inspect the active inbox.
nav.workbenchDescription: Start from what needs attention now.

portal.queueTitle: Work the inbox with clear ownership
portal.queueSubtitle: Only tickets currently assigned to you are shown here; search by subject or requester and jump straight into the thread.
portal.queueListSubtitle: Ordered by the latest activity from the API.
portal.threadSubtitle: Reply to the requester, update handling state, and preserve an auditable record.
portal.requesterSubtitle: Identity snapshot stored on the ticket.
portal.tagsSubtitle: Show rule hits and tag sources so support can verify the classification without switching back.
portal.noTags: No tags matched this ticket yet.
portal.workflowSubtitle: Update status, priority, and assignee while keeping current workload visible.

transfer.title: Request transfer
transfer.subtitle: The current assignee can request reassignment and the target assignee must accept or reject it.
transfer.reasonPlaceholder: Explain why the ticket should move, such as a bad rule match, domain mismatch, or current overload.
transfer.historySubtitle: Review reassignment requests and see who accepted, rejected, or cancelled them.

portal.feedbackSubtitle: After a ticket is finished, the requester can rate each handling support agent and leave a comment.
portal.feedbackEmpty: No feedback has been submitted yet.
portal.feedbackNoComment: The user left no written comment.
portal.replySubtitle: Support replies notify the user by message and email.
portal.replyPlaceholder: Write the next instruction, resolution step, or requested clarification.
portal.tip: Status, assignment, and replies are reflected on the user side immediately.
portal.assignmentSourceHelp: This shows how the current assignee got the ticket. Common values include smart (automatically assigned by the routing score) and manual (assigned by an administrator); -- means there is no confirmed assignee yet.
portal.escalationLevelHelp: This shows how many times the system has escalated the ticket because of SLA risk or a missed deadline. Higher numbers mean the ticket has already been reprioritized or rerouted multiple times.
portal.assignmentLockedHint: This ticket is admin-locked. Smart rerouting will not override the current assignee.
portal.workbenchTitle: Start from the tickets that need action now
portal.workbenchSubtitle: Prioritize urgent tickets, first-response risk, and the work already assigned to you without digging through reports first.

transferWorkbench.title: Transfer requests
transferWorkbench.subtitle: You have {{count}} transfer request(s) waiting for review. Confirm them early so tickets do not stall.

portal.focusLaneSubtitle: Urgent or SLA-risk tickets that should be handled first.
portal.firstResponseLaneSubtitle: Tickets still missing the first support reply.
portal.myQueueLaneSubtitle: Tickets currently assigned to you and still in motion.
portal.activeAssigneesSubtitle: Quick glance at who can still take more work.
portal.previewEmptySubtitle: Select an item from the queue to inspect its requester, SLA, and assignment before opening the full thread.
portal.ticketMetaSubtitle: Keep identity, tags, SLA, and current owner visible while you work the thread.
portal.conversationSubtitle: Read the thread first, then answer from the same workspace.
